article_title: Huaneng Connects China's Deepest Offshore Wind Farm to the Grid as 504 MW Shandong Peninsula North L Pushes Fixed-Bottom Limits to 56 Meters

content_preview.title: Huaneng Connects China's Deepest Offshore Wind Farm to the Grid as 504 MW Shandong Peninsula North L Pushes Fixed-Bottom Limits to 56 Meters
content_preview.summary: China Huaneng Group fully energized the 504 MW Shandong Peninsula North L offshore wind farm on April 7, 2026, installing 42 twelve-megawatt turbines on jacket foundations in 52 to 56 meters of water — the deepest fixed-bottom offshore wind project ever built in China.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China Huaneng Group has fully energized the 504 MW Shandong Peninsula North L offshore wind farm, the deepest commercial offshore wind installation ever built in China. Full-power grid delivery began on April 7, 2026, [according to Maritime Executive](https://maritime-executive.com/article/china-commissions-wind-farm-at-its-deepest-offshore-position), and the project is expected to generate roughly 1.7 terawatt-hours of electricity per year, displacing about 500,000 tonnes of standa...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-2 rewrite cleanly addresses both round-1 findings. Article structure unchanged (~723 words, in News range), tone still neutral and appropriately hedged on developer-supplied figures. The two corrected paragraphs (cabling + Chinese offshore wind buildout) read more rigorously than the originals.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 local snapshots from sources/2026-04/huaneng-connects-chinas-deepest-offshore-wind-farm-to-the-grid-as-504-mw-shandong-peninsula-north-l-pushes-fixed-bottom-limits-to-56-meters/ (all 200 OK). Re-verified the two specifically corrected claims: (a) 'combined technology of drones and artificial magnetic fields' for the 95.6 km submarine cable now correctly attributed to OffshoreWind.biz (verbatim in source-0); (b) '6.6 GW China / 9.2 GW global / ~72% share' attributed to GWEC verifies cleanly against source-4 (the percentage is mathematically derivable from GWEC's own 6.6/9.2 = 71.7% rounded to ~72%). Other sources (Maritime Executive, CGTN, Windtech International) carry over from round 1 verification with all attributed claims still matching.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Both round-1 misattributions resolved. The OffshoreWind.biz re-attribution is supported by the verbatim text in the snapshot. The GWEC numbers are tighter and better-sourced than the original 'roughly 6 GW / more than half'. No new factual issues introduced by the rewrite.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Unchanged from round 1 — neutral, professional, appropriately hedged. The new GWEC paragraph is more precise without becoming editorial.
editor_notes.originality: Same as round 1 — genuinely new topic, valid internal cross-references.
concerns: Four of the five sources are not on the allowlist (Maritime Executive, CGTN, Windtech International, GWEC). All are reputable trade/wire/industry-association outlets but trigger an allowlist warning. No editorial blocker.
recommendations: Optional housekeeping: add maritime-executive.com, news.cgtn.com, windtech-international.com, and gwec.net to config/source_allowlist.txt to silence future warnings on similar offshore-wind/energy coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round-2 rewrite resolves the misattributions cleanly. Clean APPROVE — ready to merge.
round_1_findings_resolved: Drone-and-magnetic-field cable technique now correctly attributed to OffshoreWind.biz (verbatim match in snapshot).
6 GW Chinese 2025 / global-additions claim replaced with sourced GWEC figures (6.6 GW China / 9.2 GW global / ~72% share).
